steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown under the "New Project" dialog.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Type ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}} into the "Application Name" input box at the top of the Visual Studio Code window in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Confirm the application name input ${{var:app_name}} by pressing Enter in the Application Name prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code editor to open the tab context menu.
Click the "Close" option in the tab context menu to close the active file in Visual Studio Code.
Click in the empty editor area of Visual Studio Code, near the center of the workspace, where no file or panel is open.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code with the ${{var:app_name}} workspace active.
Type "Notifications: Show Notifications" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the interface.
Press Enter to select the highlighted "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion There is a notification showing "Configure M365 Agents Toolkit MCP server?"
Click the "Skip" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit notification popup in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press key: f1
Type 'reload Window' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press key: enter
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code
Type "Notifications: Show Notifications" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Show Notifications" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion There is a notification showing "Configure M365 Agents Toolkit MCP server?"
Click the "Confirm" button in the M365 Agents Toolkit notification popup at the bottom right of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the ".github" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "copilot-instructions.md" file under the ".github" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
@assertion file copilot-instructions.md is opened.
Click the ".vscode" folder in the Explorer sidebar of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "mcp.json" file under the ".vscode" folder in the VS Code Explorer panel to open it.
@assertion file mcp.json is opened.
